reasoning: The legislative vehicle that would enact this change is the 2025 budget reconciliation bill. The House of Representatives already passed its version of the reconciliation bill on May 22, 2025, and the Senate Finance Committee (SFC) released its draft on June 18, 2025. Analyses of both drafts confirm that neither bill makes the existing Inflation Reduction Act domestic content requirements (which currently function as a bonus multiplier) mandatory for receiving the base Section 45Y Production Tax Credit (PTC). Instead, proposals have focused on moving up the phase-out/sunset dates for 45Y, implementing "haircuts" for direct pay (Section 6417) if domestic content isn't met, or restricting the use of "prohibited foreign entities" (which is a separate legal framework from the affirmative domestic content rules). Because making domestic content mandatory for the base 45Y credit is not in the primary drafts of either chamber and has not been significantly championed in standalone bills, it is extremely unlikely to be enacted by the end of 2025. Therefore, I assign a 5% probability.
